metadata.description: Name of the azd environment. Used to derive a unique resource token.
Primary deployment region (e.g. koreacentral).
Object id of the user running azd up. Empty when running unattended; required for first-time deploy so the operator can read state until the api takes over.
Microsoft Entra tenant id used to validate MSAL bearer tokens.
Application (client) id of the App Registration used by the SPA + api. Set after the App Registration is created (or re-used from the legacy deployment).
Frontend feature flag for the custom database builder. Set to false for production deployments that should hide this surface.
Frontend feature flag for lab tools. Set to false for production deployments that should hide this surface.
Frontend feature flag for the browser terminal. Set to false for production deployments that should hide this surface.
Comma-separated CORS allowed origins for the api ingress. Leave empty to allow same-origin only (recommended once the SPA is served by the frontend sidecar).

If true, every backing resource (Storage, Key Vault, ACR) gets publicNetworkAccess=Disabled and private endpoints. The very first deploy must keep this false so the postprovision hook can push images and seed secrets; flip to true on the second azd provision.

If true, grants the shared UAMI subscription-scope Reader so the SPA discovery wizard can list RGs / storage accounts / ACRs / VMs across the subscription. Requires the deployer to have User Access Administrator (or Owner). Set to false in restricted tenants and run the equivalent `az role assignment create` from docs/auth.md by hand.

If true, grants the shared UAMI resource-group-scope Contributor and User Access Administrator for runtime resource orchestration. Set to false when equivalent roles already exist outside this template.

If true, defines and assigns the project custom role `Elb Workload RG Creator` to the shared UAMI at subscription scope. The custom role grants ONLY `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups/{read,write,delete}` (plus sub-scope deployment reads) so AKS can auto-create its `MC_<rg>_<cluster>_<region>` node RG. The UAMI still cannot create resources inside any RG without a separate Contributor assignment at that RG scope — this is the least-privilege alternative to granting sub-scope Contributor. Disable when policy forbids any custom roles in the subscription.

Optional name of the AKS workload cluster RG (e.g. `rg-elb-cluster`). When set, grants the shared UAMI Contributor + User Access Administrator on that RG so `api.tasks.openapi.rbac.setup_workload_identity` can create `id-elb-openapi` + federated credential + downstream role assignments. Leave empty on the first `azd up` (the RG does not exist yet); set it on the second `azd provision` after the SPA has created AKS. `scripts/dev/grant-runtime-rbac.sh` is the workstation safety net for deployments that pre-date this parameter.
If true and `aksClusterResourceGroup` is non-empty, grants the shared UAMI Contributor + User Access Administrator on that RG. Set to false when equivalent roles already exist outside this template.
If true, create Application Insights. The default deployment creates only Log Analytics; Application Insights can be enabled later with ENABLE_APPLICATION_INSIGHTS=true.
Optional generated resource name slot, for example slot01 when rg-elb-dashboard already exists and should be preserved. Bicep converts slot01 to the visible -01 resource-name suffix.
Cost-center tag value used for chargeback / cost analysis filters.
Optional contact email surfaced as the `owner` tag. Leave empty to omit the tag.

variables.workspaceTags: [union(variables('tags'), createObject('elb-workload-rg', variables('rgName'), 'elb-acr-rg', variables('rgName'), 'elb-acr', variables('acrName'), 'elb-storage', variables('storageAccountName'), 'elb-region', parameters('location')))]

metadata.description: Azure region for the VNet.
Name of the VNet.
CIDR for the platform VNet. Default /20 leaves room for future subnets.
Subnet CIDR for snet-containerapps (must be /23 or larger for workload-profile env).
Subnet CIDR for snet-private-endpoints.
Subnet CIDR for snet-aks.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Azure region.
Workspace name.
Application Insights name.
If true, create Application Insights and emit its connection string. Disabled by default to keep the baseline deployment lean.

Principal id (object id) of the shared dashboard UAMI. When non-empty the workspace receives a Log Analytics Reader role assignment so the api sidecar can KQL the ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL table for the Live Wall log tail. Pass empty to skip the assignment (e.g. tests or local-only what-if runs).
Tags applied to every resource in this module.

properties.description: elb-dashboard shared UAMI — Live Wall log tail (KQL against ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL).

metadata.description: Azure region.
UAMI name.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Principal id (object id) of the shared UAMI to grant Reader to.

properties.description: elb-dashboard shared UAMI — sub-scope Reader for SPA discovery wizard.

metadata.description: Principal id (object id) of the shared UAMI used by the api/worker sidecars.

properties.description: elb-dashboard shared UAMI — create and manage runtime resources in this RG.
elb-dashboard shared UAMI — assign runtime RBAC for AKS kubelet/workload identities.

metadata.description: Principal id (object id) of the shared UAMI to grant the custom role to.
Stable token appended to the role definition name so multiple environments in the same subscription do not collide.

variables.roleDescription: Allows the dashboard managed identity to create / read / delete subscription resource groups so AKS can auto-create its MC_* node RG, and to assign a fixed whitelist of runtime roles (Contributor, User Access Administrator, Storage Blob Data Contributor, AcrPull, AKS Cluster User) so it can self-heal RBAC on the workload cluster RG.

properties.description: elb-dashboard shared UAMI — sub-scope RG-write for AKS MC_* auto-create + ABAC-restricted self-heal of runtime RBAC.

metadata.description: Principal id (object id) of the shared UAMI used by the api/worker sidecars.

properties.description: elb-dashboard shared UAMI — create id-elb-openapi MI + federated cred + read/list AKS in the workload RG.
elb-dashboard shared UAMI — assign Contributor + AKS Cluster User to id-elb-openapi in the workload RG.

metadata.description: Azure region.
Globally-unique ACR name (5-50 chars, alphanumeric).
Principal id of the UAMI that needs AcrPull on this registry.
Resource id of the snet-private-endpoints subnet.
Resource id of the platform VNet (used to link the private DNS zone).
If true, leaves publicNetworkAccess=Enabled until the postprovision image build finishes. Set to false in steady state to lock the registry to the VNet.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Azure region.
Globally-unique storage account name (3-24 lowercase chars).
Resource id of the snet-private-endpoints subnet.
Resource id of the platform VNet (used to link private DNS zones).
Principal id of the UAMI that needs data-plane RBAC on this account.
If true, allows the operator workstation to bootstrap state during first deploy by leaving public access enabled. Steady state must be false.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Name of the existing platform Storage account.
Azure region.
Globally-unique Key Vault name (3-24 chars).
AAD tenant id.
Principal id of the UAMI that needs Secrets User on this vault.
Object id of the operator running azd up. Granted Secrets Officer when non-empty.
Resource id of the snet-private-endpoints subnet.
Resource id of the platform VNet (used to link the private DNS zone).
If true, leaves publicNetworkAccess=Enabled during first deploy so the operator can seed secrets. Steady state must be false.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Azure region for the environment.
Name for the Container Apps Environment.
Customer id of the platform Log Analytics workspace.
Resource id of the platform Log Analytics workspace.
Resource id of the snet-containerapps subnet (delegated to Microsoft.App/environments).
If true, ingress is internal-only (no public IP). Phase 1 keeps this false.
Tags applied to every resource in this module.
Azure region.
Container App name (e.g. ca-elb-dashboard).
Resource id of the Container Apps Environment.
Login server for the platform ACR.
Tag of the api image in the platform ACR.
Tag of the frontend image in the platform ACR.
Tag of the terminal image in the platform ACR.
If true, all sidecars boot from a public hello-world image. Postprovision hook flips this to false via az containerapp update.
Resource id of the user-assigned managed identity shared by all sidecars.
Client id of the same UAMI (used by azure-identity inside the containers).
Principal/object id of the same UAMI (used for runtime RBAC assignments).
AAD tenant id used to validate MSAL bearer tokens.
App Registration client id (audience) for the api.
Frontend feature flag for the custom database builder. Set to false to hide menu entries and route access.
Frontend feature flag for lab tools. Set to false to hide menu entries and route access.
Frontend feature flag for the browser terminal. Set to false to hide menu entries, dashboard card, shortcuts, and route access.
App Insights connection string for telemetry from inside the containers.

Log Analytics workspace id (customerId GUID). Used by the api sidecar to KQL `ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL` for the Live Wall log tail when the historical project-local log files are not available (i.e. always, in deployment). Empty disables the LA fallback and the Live Wall log tiles stay blank.
Platform Storage account name (used to derive the table endpoint for jobstate / jobhistory access).
Resource id of the platform subnet where workload Storage private endpoints are created for api/worker/terminal access.
Subscription id (passed into the api/worker env vars so monitor routes can default subscription_id when not provided in the query string).
CORS allowed origins for the api ingress. Empty list disables CORS (same-origin only).
Tags applied to every resource in this module.

Shared secret used by the api / worker sidecars to authenticate with the terminal exec server (loopback :7682). Auto-rotated on every deployment via newGuid(); both sidecars receive the same Container Apps secret reference so they always agree.
